cards.title: About this column
cards.description: The Backlog holds tasks you want to do eventually but haven't started yet. Drop ideas here and prioritize them when you're ready.

cards.title: About this column
cards.description: Cards you move here get picked up by the task executor and run automatically using Claude Code. The executor checks this column every hour. When a task completes, Claude moves it to Review so you can check the output before marking it done.

cards.title: About this column
cards.description: Tasks currently in progress. When you or the AI starts working on something, it moves here.

cards.title: About this column
cards.description: When the AI cannot do something on its own, it puts a card here. These tasks require human action: approvals, logins, phone calls, or anything that needs your personal touch.

cards.title: About this column
cards.description: Completed tasks that need your review before they're considered done. Check the work, approve it, and move it to Done.

cards.title: About this column
cards.description: Finished tasks. Everything here has been completed and verified.

cards.title: About this column
cards.description: Ideas on ice. Tasks you've decided to shelve for now, not deleted, just paused until you're ready.

cards.title: Tips: Getting started
cards.description: Double-click any card to edit it. Drag cards between columns to change their status. Use the + button at the top to add new columns. Cards can be assigned to Projects to group related work together.

cards.title: Set up automated email welcome sequence
cards.description: Create a 5-email drip sequence that triggers when a new subscriber joins. Include welcome, value delivery, and call-to-action emails.

cards.title: Build client onboarding checklist
cards.description: Design a step-by-step onboarding flow: intake form, welcome call scheduling, resource sharing, and first milestone check-in.

cards.title: Research my top 3 competitors
cards.description:
Search Google for "[your industry] competitors" and "[your main product or service] alternatives". Find the 3 competitors that appear most often across results.

For each competitor:
1. Visit their website and find their pricing page
2. List every pricing tier: name, monthly price, what is included at each level
3. Copy their homepage headline word for word
4. Find one customer review that mentions their biggest weakness
5. Write 2 sentences on how they are positioned differently from me

Save the results as competitor-research.md on my Desktop. Use one section per competitor with a clear header.

cards.title: Write 7 Instagram captions from my most recent long-form content
cards.description:
Find my most recent blog post, podcast transcript, or recording on my Desktop or in ~/Documents. Read through it and identify the 7 strongest insights, opinions, or story moments.

For each one, write an Instagram caption that:
1. Opens with the insight as the very first line — no "Have you ever..." or "I want to talk about..."
2. Expands on it in 3 to 4 short sentences
3. Ends with a question that invites a comment
4. Stays under 150 words total
5. Does not include hashtags

Number them 1 through 7. For each caption, note which part of the original content it came from. Save as captions.md on my Desktop.

cards.title: Research the top Claude Code skills for small business owners
cards.description:
Search GitHub and the web for the best Claude Code skills that coaches, consultants, and small business owners are using right now. Focus on skills for: email writing, social media content, client follow-ups, research, and business automation.

For each skill you find:
1. Name of the skill and where to get it (GitHub URL or install command)
2. What it does in one plain-English sentence
3. Who it is most useful for (what type of business or use case)
4. How to install it (the exact command or steps)
5. A rating from 1 to 5 on how useful it would be for a coach or consultant

Find at least 5 skills. Sort them by rating. Save as claude-skills.md on my Desktop.

cards.title: AI system check — write a file to my Desktop
cards.description:
Create a file called ai-test.md on my Desktop.

Write the following into it:

# AI Executor Test
**Date:** [today's date and time]
**Status:** Working

## 5 Business Automation Ideas for Claude Code

For each idea, write: the name, one sentence on what it does, and one sentence on how to build it with Claude Code.

Pick 5 ideas relevant to coaches, consultants, or online business owners.

---
*Generated by Claude Code via Mission Control Task Executor*

Save the file. Write DONE on the last line of your response.

cards.title: Find 10 qualified leads in my target market
cards.description:
Search LinkedIn (or Google for LinkedIn profiles) for people who match all of these criteria:
- Job title includes: founder, CEO, owner, or director
- Industry: [fill in your target industry before moving this card to AI column]
- Company size: between 5 and 50 employees
- Location: [your target region]

For each of the 10 leads:
1. Full name
2. Current title and company name
3. LinkedIn profile URL
4. One specific detail from their profile that suggests they might need what I offer
5. A suggested opening line for a first message — not a pitch, just a genuine conversation starter

Save as leads.md on my Desktop.

cards.title: Record intro video for my program
cards.description: Film a 3-minute welcome video introducing yourself and what participants will get from this program. The AI cannot record video for you — this one is yours.

cards.title: Review AI-drafted email sequence
cards.description: Claude drafted 5 email templates for the welcome sequence. Read through each one, adjust the tone to match your voice, and approve before they go live.

cards.title: Configure domain and SSL
cards.description: Domain pointed, SSL certificate installed, all pages loading over HTTPS.

cards.title: Build mobile app version
cards.description: Future idea: create a companion app for managing tasks on the go. Not a priority right now.

cards.title: Go research one of my competitors from mastermindsHQ.business and give me a pop-up modal window that shows the information and also allows me to close it.
Card title: AI system check
cards.description:
Description:                                                    
                                                                  
  Create a file called ai-test.md on my Desktop.
                                                                  
  Write the following into it:
                                                                  
  # AI Executor Test         
  **Date:** [today's date and time]            
                                                                  
  ## 5 Business Automation Ideas for Claude Code
                                                                  
  For each idea, write: the name, one sentence on what it does,
  and one sentence on how to build it with Claude Code.           
                                                       
  Pick 5 ideas relevant to coaches, consultants, or online        
  business owners.

Email Automation System.description: SAMPLE: Automated email sequences and drip campaigns for subscriber onboarding and conversion. Replace this with your own email automation project.

Client Onboarding Portal.description: SAMPLE: End-to-end onboarding flow for new coaching clients including intake, booking, and welcome automation. Customize this for your own client intake process.

Content Strategy Hub.description: SAMPLE: Blog posts, lead magnets, and content calendar for growing organic reach and authority. Adapt this to your own content marketing strategy.

Revenue Growth Tracker.description: SAMPLE: Dashboard and workflows to track monthly recurring revenue, churn, and growth levers. Use this as a template for your own financial tracking.

Infrastructure Setup.description: SAMPLE: Core tech stack configuration including Stripe, booking system, CRM, and automation integrations. This is the foundation everything else runs on.
